trivia_bullets.text: The 'Stoneforge' name is a deliberate echo of Stoneforge Mystic — one of the most powerful Equipment tutors ever printed (banned in Modern, restricted in Legacy). Stoneforge Acolyte's Cohort version of the same ability (look at top 4, find Equipment) is a significantly weaker version gated behind the Ally synergy requirement. The naming is a flavor connection: the Kor's tradition of crafting and wielding magical weapons stretches across multiple Zendikar sets. [Scryfall]

Flavor text: "The stone speaks. We are merely interpreters." [Scryfall] The line frames the Kor forge-craft as receptive rather than creative — the craftsperson listens to the material rather than imposing a design on it. This is consistent with Kor culture across Zendikar story materials, where craft and spiritual practice are intertwined.

Cohort Equipment tutor: tap this and an untapped Ally to look at the top 4 cards and pull an Equipment card to hand. The ability is uncommon-only and has no reprint — it's a single-printing card. EDHREC rank 22471. In Pauper Commander, Stoneforge Acolyte has been used as a commander for voltron-style Equipment builds, exploiting its built-in Equipment-search as a repeatable value engine. [Scryfall; WebSearch: moxfield.com Pauper EDH deck snippet]
